primary_reason: This retrospective comparison evaluates surgical approaches for posterior cruciate ligament reconstruction and provides no infectious-disease transfer route.
This systematic review and re-analysis compares vaccine-preventable disease incidence across clinical, radiological, and etiological outcome definitions.
This qualitative triangulation examines pandemic-related work and personal impacts among disabled employees rather than infectious-disease outcomes.
This systematic ClinicalTrials.gov analysis programmatically and manually assesses language-based eligibility restrictions, including infectious-disease trials.
This HIV PMTCT cohort compares community-facility linkage models using record review, biomarker-supported imputation, and longitudinal maternal and infant outcomes.
This review critically discusses diagnostic differentiation of EBV-associated gastrointestinal lymphoproliferative disorders and lymphomas.
This review provides a diagnostic overview of EBV-associated gastrointestinal lymphoid disorders with overlapping morphology and immunophenotypes.
This minireview summarizes biological evidence on cholesterol trafficking in viral infection without developing or evaluating an epidemiologic method or design.
This review offers a practical diagnostic approach to EBV-associated gastrointestinal lymphoid disorders, including post-transplant disease.
This factorial cluster randomized trial evaluates withdrawal of financial incentives and audit-feedback for chlamydia testing in primary care.
This paper identifies non-generalizable serologic sensitivity estimates as a source of bias in SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence correction.
This review concerns microbiome mechanisms in psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease without an infectious-disease methodological application.
This large vaccine-safety study combines passive VAERS reports and active v-safe monitoring to characterize adverse events after COVID-19 vaccination.
This study triangulates population surveys and laboratory sentinel data to estimate chlamydia prevalence and assess screening targeting.
This is a protocol for a future mixed-methods study of maternal morbidity and mortality rather than a completed methodological resource.
This genomic analysis evaluates the population generalizability of actionable genes for malaria, tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS, and sickle cell disease.
This letter replies to commentary on post-cure HCV hepatocellular carcinoma prediction without providing an abstract or substantive method content.
This household-level serosurvey uses multilevel models and assay-performance adjustment to study structural SARS-CoV-2 infection risks in a Brazilian slum.
This nationwide stepped-wedge time-series analysis estimates the association between UV-C terminal cleaning implementation and hospital-onset gram-negative bloodstream infections.
This randomized trial compares malaria chemoprevention regimens in Kenyan children with sickle cell anemia using clinical and parasitologic outcomes.
This qualitative literature synthesis categorizes vaccine trust constructs and their proposed relationships with COVID-19 vaccine acceptance.
This implementation study describes and evaluates an immunization data-triangulation process and decision-support tool linking program and vaccine-supply data.
This multiverse analysis evaluates whether combinations of demographic covariates reduce sampling bias in online COVID-19 surveys.
This study combines cohort analyses with simulations to quantify how selection mechanisms can bias COVID-19 infection and prognosis estimates.
This paper develops and benchmarks an optimized metaheuristic image-segmentation algorithm using general and COVID-19 chest radiograph images.
This case study details implementation of stakeholder-led data triangulation and dashboard development for COVID-19 vaccine adverse-event surveillance in Nigeria.
This multicenter study develops and independently externally validates a CT artificial-intelligence model for COVID-19 extent and CO-RADS classification.
This propensity-score weighted cohort study estimates associations between COVID-19 symptoms or infection and later suicidal ideation without a transferable infectious-disease design contribution.
This efficient cluster randomized trial evaluates a multifaceted prognostic and stewardship intervention for childhood respiratory infections in primary care.
This individual-participant meta-analysis compares tuberculosis diagnostic yields while explicitly accounting for specimen availability and harmonized denominators.
This randomized trial compares same-day versus delayed HIV or tuberculosis treatment pathways for people presenting with TB symptoms at HIV diagnosis.
This paper develops and evaluates a deep-learning and Delaunay-triangulation system for COVID-19 diagnosis from chest radiographs.
This bioinformatics analysis identifies shared expression signatures between lupus and COVID-19 but does not validate a diagnostic or transferable epidemiologic method.
This individual-participant meta-analysis studies gestational weight gain in low- and middle-income countries without an infectious-disease application or concrete transfer route.
This nested cluster-randomized study evaluates menstrual cups using repeated bacterial-vaginosis, vaginal-microbiome, and sexually transmitted infection measurements.
This scoping review compares enrolled and real-world bloodstream-infection trial populations and prescribes approaches to improve external validity.
This study evaluates external transportability and multisite learning for bacterial-infection prediction models across tertiary and community ICUs.
This paper develops g-computation and inverse-probability-weighting estimators that synthesize statistical and simulation models under transportability positivity violations.
This review addresses HLA, gut microbiota, and autoimmune comorbidity rather than infectious-disease methods or outcomes.
This analysis triangulates routine antenatal, fertility, and facility data to correct biased HIV prevalence and Spectrum estimates in Mozambique.
This study develops and assesses inverse-probability weighting strategies for improving external validity of Clostridioides difficile prediction models.
This systematic review categorizes quantitative methods for defining RSV epidemic timing and provides a framework for method selection and interpretation.
This review quantifies demographic reporting and representation in randomized antibacterial trials for Staphylococcus aureus infections.
This national surveillance analysis triangulates laboratory and cohort data to estimate cryptococcal meningitis incidence as an HIV-program success metric.
This pragmatic randomized trial compares directly observed, self-administered, and shared-choice delivery strategies for tuberculosis preventive treatment among people with HIV.

This meta-epidemiological study tests whether features causing generalizability bias in preliminary trials also inflate effects across several health-behavior fields including sexually transmitted infection behaviors.
This multicountry retrospective cohort evaluates long-term HIV retention, viral-load monitoring, and suppression before and after universal-treatment guideline adoption.
This study develops antimicrobial-resistance machine-learning models from English genomes and evaluates their generalizability on independent African Escherichia coli datasets.
This study triangulates a prospective breast-cancer cohort and Mendelian-randomization analyses to assess whether G-CSF worsens COVID-19 outcomes.
This study triangulates deep-learning classified Twitter vaccine rhetoric, self-reported acceptance, and vaccination uptake to assess behavioral surveillance validity.
The study characterizes and stabilizes Langya-virus glycoprotein antigens for vaccine development but does not contribute an epidemiological method.
This paper develops post-selection inference for multiverse analyses, proves error control, and illustrates the framework with COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy data.
This cluster-randomized trial sub-study measures longitudinal gut resistance determinants after differing histories of mass azithromycin distribution in Niger.
This multicountry impact evaluation and microsimulation forecasts how primary-health-care coverage affects child mortality, including vaccine-preventable conditions and infectious gastroenteritis.
This legal-policy commentary on firearms in hospitals contains no infectious-disease methodological, diagnostic, or surveillance contribution.
This implementation resource describes capacity building and indicator-based triangulation of immunization, surveillance, and coverage data to find vaccination inequities.
This paper proposes and evaluates a case-definition cutoff criterion that minimizes absolute-risk bias under nondifferential outcome misclassification, illustrated with postsurgical infections.
This large linked Brazilian cohort uses inverse-probability weighting and sensitivity triangulation to estimate primary health care effects on AIDS incidence and mortality.
This comparative study evaluates marginal standardization and raking for generalizing pediatric SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence estimates to a target population.
This paper details an inverse-probability-weighted data-fusion method and diagnostic for bridged treatment comparisons, illustrated with HIV trials.
This influenza-vaccination study triangulates difference-in-differences estimates with an age-structured transmission model and probabilistic bias analyses.
This systematic review investigates molecular commonality among noninfectious neoplasms without an infectious-disease transfer route.
This EBV-relevant lymphoma study uses a cell model, three-dimensional nanotechnology, and patient-sample confirmation to characterize mechanistic telomere disruption.
This validation study adapts and assesses feasibility, sensitivity, specificity, and reliability of an asymptomatic-bacteriuria stewardship metric in critical-access hospitals.
This review proposes a mechanistic triangulation for neurodegeneration, inflammation, and demyelination in neurological disease without an infectious-disease application.
The work engineers protein nanocages for antigen display and delivery, which is vaccine-product construction without an epidemiological method or infectious process.
The study develops structure-based neural prediction of MHC-bound peptides for immunology and vaccine design rather than infectious epidemiology.
This comparative study standardizes venous-hemodynamic assessment for chronic venous disease treatment and has no infectious-disease transfer route.
This study develops, evaluates, and externally validates a calculator-free emergency-department score for predicting ICU admission in COVID-19.
This policy review and youth-interview triangulation concerns vaping prevention rather than an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete infectious transfer route.
This bioinformatics analysis studies nephrolithiasis and hyperlipidemia without an infectious-disease application or concrete transfer route.
This methodological paper defines immune-selection stability of causal risk ratios and explains its use for transportability arguments across populations.
This review summarizes CD46 biology and cancer-therapy interactions, with pathogen receptor status as background rather than a methodological infectious-disease contribution.
This commentary critiques a study of post-splenectomy complications and provides no infectious-disease methods content.
This vaccine-safety evaluation triangulates passive, acute-flaccid-paralysis, hospital-based, and prospective cohort surveillance after Uganda's nOPV2 campaign.
This review discusses proposed shared pathogenic mechanisms of Helicobacter pylori and Porphyromonas gingivalis without developing or evaluating a transferable epidemiologic method.
This proof-of-concept corrects SARS-CoV-2 incidence denominators with mobile-phone population data to account for seasonal mobility.
This study develops and cross-cohort evaluates causal Bayesian-network models for identifying clinical trajectories preceding CID/CVID diagnosis.
This multicountry fixed-effects impact evaluation and microsimulation forecasting estimates how aid funding affects mortality from HIV, malaria, tuberculosis, and other infectious causes.
This systematic review compares predictive-model performance, validation, calibration, and generalizability for surgical-site infection prediction.
This mixed-methods triangulation evaluates provider adherence to malaria diagnosis and treatment standards using records review, interviews, and focus groups.
This propensity-weighted cohort study examines preconception hypoglycemia and pregnancy outcomes rather than an infectious-disease outcome or transferable infectious-disease method.
This survey provides a systematic taxonomy of societal digital-twin approaches for infection containment and discusses their validation and design limitations.
This preprint reports the same Senegal malaria mass-drug-administration transportability analysis as the subsequently published primary record.
This mechanistic infection study shows that Pseudomonas aeruginosa alters artificial-mucus rheology and transport through metalloproteinase-associated mucin degradation.
This multicentre study demonstrates standardized ICU CLABSI surveillance with denominator collection, quality assurance, and resistance profiling.
This multicentre validation evaluates the reliability and diagnostic accuracy of the MSKI-RADS classification for musculoskeletal infections.
This correspondence comments on the applicability of an existing surgical-site-infection prediction model without supplying method content.
This narrative review proposes biological links among EBV, vitamin D, hepcidin, and multiple sclerosis without a transferable epidemiologic method.
This observational cohort reports adjusted growth differences between HIV-exposed and unexposed infants without demonstrating a reusable design beyond standard regression.
This study adapts survey nonresponse weighting to correct inactive-record inflation in health-care-database population denominators.
This meta-analysis concerns microbiome associations with psychological well-being and does not establish a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This review concerns weed-control strategies in tea plantations rather than an infectious-disease application with a concrete transferable method.
This hepatitis-B application combines microbiome clustering, predictive signatures, and Mendelian-randomization triangulation to investigate disease activity and causality.
The preprint develops a structural graph-attention model for SARS-CoV-2 protein-interface prediction rather than epidemiological inference.
This study develops and cross-platform evaluates interpretable MALDI-TOF machine-learning classifiers for carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae while assessing generalizability limits.
This paper introduces GPAT, a gene-perturbation framework for evaluating transportability of in-vitro models to in-vivo phenotypes, including antiviral-response models.
This reanalysis compares variable-selection methods, stability selection, and triangulation to reduce overfitting in infectious lameness risk-factor studies.
This study estimates national hepatitis-C prevalence and clearance from residual sera using stratified sampling, reflex testing, and raking adjustment.
This study applies energy-landscape analysis to psychological distress during COVID-19 rather than to an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer application.
This panel-data impact evaluation and microsimulation forecast estimates how development assistance affects mortality from major communicable diseases.
This immunization-registry evaluation tests a dormant-record rule to correct denominator inflation and improve vaccination-coverage estimates.
This study develops and prospectively multicentre validates an explainable machine-learning diagnostic model for pediatric abdominal sepsis.
This study triangulates cohort, surveillance, treatment-sales, and literature data to estimate HIV cohort coverage and representativeness.
This household serosurvey compares intervention and matched nonintervention communities to evaluate autocidal gravid ovitrap effectiveness against Zika infection.
This cross-country hospital survey describes existing levels of automated healthcare-associated-infection surveillance without evaluating a transferable surveillance method.
This paper develops causal-inference interpretation and transportability considerations for infectious exposures with unmeasured strain-specific treatment versions.
This study uses machine-learning transportability models to map where trial-estimated malaria mass-drug-administration effects generalize across Senegalese communes.
This paper develops high-content morphologic pseudotime analysis to reconstruct single-cell EBV reactivation and host DNA-damage-response dynamics from snapshot data.
This multi-omics Mendelian-randomization study concerns IgA nephropathy and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
The article discusses empirical-reference complications for B-cell epitope prediction in vaccine design but does not contribute a transferable infectious-epidemiology method.
This multicentre neonatal benchmarking program uses standardized data to compare outcomes and identify high rates of late-onset nosocomial infection.
This paper develops and compares agent-based simulations of chronic viral infection with a mathematical model to assess reproducibility and parameter effects.
This paper calibrates an agent-based Leishmania infection model with Gaussian-process sensitivity analysis and biological observations.
This project implements standardized prospective ICU infection surveillance and web-based benchmarking across an Italian hospital network.
This stochastic food-chain simulation evaluates intervention combinations to reduce verocytotoxigenic Escherichia coli contamination in ground beef.
This study develops a NetLogo agent-based model of host inflammatory signaling and tests infectious and noninfectious perturbation scenarios.
This prospective ICU registry study establishes benchmarks for empiric antibiotic use and quantifies how often treatment follows confirmed infection.
This study benchmarks recombinant adeno-associated-virus vector infectivity for gene therapy rather than infectious-disease epidemiology.
The review addresses benchmarking of B-cell epitope prediction for peptide-vaccine design without a transferable epidemiological design contribution.
This paper proposes a mechanistic joint model linking nonlinear HIV-RNA and CD4 dynamics with time to clinical progression.
This diagnostic-specificity study concerns procalcitonin in aseptic carcinoma patients rather than an infectious-disease outcome or transferable infectious application.

This practice resource describes national hand-hygiene implementation strategies, surveillance indicators, compliance audits, and benchmarking approaches for healthcare-associated-infection prevention.
This simulation models competitive coexistence of tick species rather than infectious-disease transmission or an identified transferable infectious application.
This surgical-service benchmark study evaluates herniotomy complications rather than infectious-disease outcomes or methods.
This agent-based simulation reconstructs 1918 influenza spread across small communities and tests the effects of settlement structure and mobility.
This stochastic hospital-transmission simulation compares antibiotic-control protocols for reducing healthcare-associated infections and resistance.
This editorial comments on on-treatment benchmarks for chronic hepatitis B without presenting sufficient method or guidance content.
This paper refines and scales an agent-based HIV model to represent gastrointestinal heterogeneity and assess its effect on disease progression.
This mechanistic sheep-nematode model incorporates acquired immunity and calibrates parasite dynamics to observed faecal egg-count patterns.
This paper links a mechanistic plant-pathogen model to a simpler compartmental model to study grapevine growth effects on powdery-mildew epidemics.
This evaluation compares visual inspection, ATP bioluminescence, and microbiological screening to derive provisional hospital-cleanliness monitoring benchmarks.
This study builds synthetic age-specific contact matrices from individual activity data and validates them against varicella and parvovirus serology.
This paper derives an analytically tractable approach for stochastic traveling-wave models applicable to epidemic spread and microbial population dynamics.
This paper uses an agent-based immune simulator and dynamic network analysis to identify cellular interaction patterns associated with viral clearance.
This direct-observation study estimates setting-specific hand-hygiene opportunities to supply denominators for healthcare-associated-infection prevention monitoring.
This stochastic within-herd model evaluates how batch management and room decontamination alter Salmonella prevalence at slaughter.
This agent-based model compares individual and combined livestock, dog, vaccination, and education strategies for cystic echinococcosis control.
This review proposes patient-level HIV surveillance feedback and performance benchmarks to guide linkage-to-care and retention service delivery.
This spatially explicit agent-based model represents human-environment interactions and evaluates schistosomiasis control strategies in an endemic village.
This paper develops and validates an individual-based framework that jointly represents influenza transmission, ecology, and strain evolution.
This national ICU surveillance analysis estimates changes in catheter-associated urinary-tract-infection rates after participation in standardized benchmarking.
This review compares healthcare-associated-infection benchmarking and public-reporting systems and discusses their methodological limitations and policy effects.
This prospective occupational-exposure surveillance study benchmarks percutaneous injuries against a multinational hospital network to inform prevention.
This spatial agent-based simulation evaluates continuous and discontinuous weekend-extension policies for mitigating seasonal and pandemic influenza outbreaks.
This diagnostic-accuracy study evaluates a biomarker for unhealthy alcohol use among people with HIV rather than an infectious outcome or infectious diagnostic method.
This activity-based agent simulation compares household quarantine, school closure, and social-activity restrictions for urban influenza control.
This simulation study evaluates random-regression methods for estimating genetic tolerance to infection and identifies data structures that produce bias.
This agent-based emergency-department model simulates influenza-like-illness spread and compares patient- and staff-focused infection-control policies.
This paper develops a case-mix-adjusted indirect-standardization model for benchmarking hospital antibiotic use.
